rxn_class_summary: Photoredox/nickel dual-catalyzed three-component HAT-mediated dicarbofunctionalization (DCF) of alkenes, coupling an aryl halide, an activated alkene, and a C-H precursor (ether/alcohol/amide) via a diaryl ketone HAT catalyst.

key_intermediates.name: Excited triplet diaryl ketone (benzophenone derivative) diradical II
Carbon-centered alkyl radical IV (alpha to heteroatom from C-H precursor)
Giese radical adduct VI (alkyl radical after addition to activated alkene)
Ni(III) intermediate X/E (productive tetrahedral, after radical capture)

electron_flow: Photoexcited diaryl ketone (triplet) abstracts a hydrogen atom from the C-H precursor to give a ketyl radical and an alpha-heteroatom alkyl radical; this radical undergoes Giese addition to the activated alkene, and the resulting stabilized radical is captured by a Ni(0)/Ni(II) species, oxidative addition/isomerization gives a Ni(III) aryl-alkyl intermediate that reductively eliminates the DCF product, and SET from the ketyl radical to Ni(I) regenerates both ground-state catalysts.
rate_determining_step: Selectivity-determining (kinetic) step is the competition between radical metalation to tetrahedral 3C (A-TS-B', 11.5 kcal/mol) versus Giese addition to the acrylate (A-TS-D, ~isoenergetic); HAT from C-H precursor by triplet ketone is the productive radical-forming step.

probe_reagents.name: i-PrOH-d7 (deuterated isopropanol)
probe_reagents.observation: Intermolecular competition i-PrOH vs i-PrOH-d7 gave 3.6:1 ratio of 40 to 40-d6, consistent with exothermic radical C-H abstraction

probe_reagents.name: Irradiation control without benzophenone catalyst 1
probe_reagents.observation: No DCF product formed at 390 or 300 nm in absence of 1, ruling out direct alkene photoexcitation or bromide photolysis

probe_reagents.name: Ni(dtbbpy)(NO3)2 with 4-iodobenzonitrile (no Br source)
probe_reagents.observation: Good yield of DCF product obtained, ruling out bromide-radical-mediated HAT

probe_reagents.name: 1-Cyclopropylethanol (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Major product (77, 44%) is two-component ring-opened cross-coupling with acrylonitrile; with t-Bu acrylate, cyclopropane-containing DCF 75 (38%) and [3+2] adduct 76 (15%) observed, evidencing alpha-cyclopropyl radical ring opening competitive with Giese addition

probe_reagents.name: CPME (cyclopentyl methyl ether) selectivity probe
probe_reagents.observation: Innate HAT selectivity favors secondary alpha-alkoxy radical; minor primary alpha-alkoxy radical CC byproduct observed

probe_reagents.name: MgCl2 (chaotrope to disrupt H-bonding)
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of MgCl2 restored high selectivity for hydroalkylation of acrylonitrile vs adamantyl acrylate with i-PrOH, attributed to disruption of H-bond between i-PrOH and acrylate

notes: Two competing Ni pathways were computed: blue pathway (radical addition to singlet square-planar 1C at axial position, A-TS-B, 12.8 kcal/mol) versus favored green pathway (radical addition to triplet tetrahedral 3C at equatorial position, A-TS-B', 11.5 kcal/mol). Alternative Ni(0)/Ni(I)/Ni(III) and Ni(0)/Ni(II)/Ni(III) cycles are both energetically feasible and converge on singlet 1C. DCF vs two-component CC selectivity is kinetically controlled by the relative energies of metalation (A-TS-B') vs Giese addition (A-TS-D), strongly modulated by hydrogen bonding between alpha-hydroxy radicals and acrylate carbonyl (locks hydroxyl orientation in Ni(III) intermediate 66 toward C=O, favoring reductive elimination/DCF). Tertiary radicals favor exclusive Giese/DCF (no direct metalation). DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVPP//(U)B3LYP-D3/def2-SVP-CPCM(benzene) used for energetics.
